title: Lab: DHCP DORA Process Analysis
description:
Analyze a complete DHCP lease lifecycle including the DORA process (Discover → Offer → Request → Acknowledge), gratuitous ARP for duplicate address detection, lease renewal, and release. Understand broadcast vs unicast behavior and DHCP option fields.

Use the PCAP viewer below to examine each DHCP packet and answer the analysis questions.
hints: The client starts with IP 0.0.0.0 because it has no address yet
DORA = Discover, Offer, Request, Acknowledge — the four message types
Broadcast (ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff) is used when the client can't receive unicast
The T1 timer (50% of lease) triggers unicast renewal; T2 (87.5%) triggers broadcast renewal

labQuestions.question: Why does the DHCP Discover packet (packet #1) use source IP 0.0.0.0?
options: It's a reserved loopback address
The client has no IP address yet — it hasn't been assigned one
0.0.0.0 is the DHCP server's address
It's the default gateway address
labQuestions.correctAnswer: The client has no IP address yet — it hasn't been assigned one

labQuestions.question: What IP address does the DHCP server offer to the client?
Why is the DHCP Request (packet #3) sent as a broadcast even though the client already knows the server's IP?
options: The client's network stack requires broadcast for all DHCP messages
Broadcasting informs ALL DHCP servers which offer was accepted (so others can reclaim their offers)
Unicast is not supported by DHCP
The client hasn't configured its IP yet so it can only broadcast
labQuestions.correctAnswer: Broadcasting informs ALL DHCP servers which offer was accepted (so others can reclaim their offers)

labQuestions.question: What is the lease time offered by the DHCP server?
options: 3600 seconds (1 hour)
43200 seconds (12 hours)
86400 seconds (24 hours)
604800 seconds (7 days)

labQuestions.question: What is the purpose of the gratuitous ARP in packet #5?
options: To find the MAC address of the DHCP server
To check if the assigned IP (192.168.1.150) is already in use by another device
To announce the client's presence to the router
To request a different IP address
labQuestions.correctAnswer: To check if the assigned IP (192.168.1.150) is already in use by another device

labQuestions.question: Compare packets #1 (initial DORA) and #6 (renewal). What key difference shows that #6 is a renewal?
options: Renewal uses TCP instead of UDP
Renewal is unicast (directly to server) instead of broadcast, and ciaddr is set
Renewal uses a different Transaction ID
Renewal doesn't include the hostname option
labQuestions.correctAnswer: Renewal is unicast (directly to server) instead of broadcast, and ciaddr is set

labQuestions.question: What DNS servers are assigned to the client via DHCP Option 6?
At what percentage of the lease time does the T1 (renewal) timer expire?
labQuestions.hint: T1 = 43200s is exactly half of the 86400s lease time
